title: lachemilla repens
intro: Lachemilla repens, also known as creeping lady's mantle or creeping pimpernel, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Lachemilla repens include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
